explanation: Contradictory statements detected from the same speaker across turns
conversation_text: Hey B, so uh, about that new marketing push the CEO mentioned in the all-hands last week. He said we gotta double down on TikTok because 'that's where the eyeballs are.' What do you think our strategy should be? Yeah, I remember that. CEO was super clear—'TikTok is non-negotiable, it's exploding with Gen Z.' So, we should probably allocate, like, 60% of the budget there, right? He hates when we spread thin. Totally, he did say spreading thin is for amateurs. But wait, didn't he also rant about Instagram Reels being a copycat? Or was that something else? Anyway, yeah, heavy on TikTok. Maybe hire some influencers? Influencers? Oh man, he loves that. Remember when he said 'micro-influencers are gold, they convert better than big shots'? We gotta find ones with under 50k followers in our niche. No debates. Haha, yeah, he went off on that one. 'Big influencers are just billboards, micros are word-of-mouth.' Okay, so prioritize micros. But budget— he said 'don't skimp on paid ads,' right? Even if it hurts short-term.
